plan.title: Add persistent sub-agent backend policy and availability gate

narratives.Description: This story gives Deft a durable project-level policy for the user-selected coding sub-agent backend and a runtime capability probe that lists available backends in the current harness. It is independently buildable because it owns the policy/task/probe surface; the later launch story consumes that policy before dispatch.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the source files scripts/policy.py, scripts/policy_set.py, and tasks/policy.yml so plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend can be shown, changed, and audited from the normal policy surface.
2. Add focused tests in tests/cli/test_policy.py and tests/cli/test_policy_set.py that verify backend listing/probe output, user-changeable policy storage, and policy:show evidence without invoking a real harness.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want my selected coding sub-agent backend stored in project policy and checked against the current harness, so that swarm stops and asks me when the configured agent is unavailable.

items.title: Given the user changes the backend through the policy task surface, then the selected value is stored under plan.policy in PROJECT-DEFINITION and task policy:show reports the current value and source.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the user changes the backend through the policy task surface, then the selected value is stored under plan.policy in PROJECT-DEFINITION and task policy:show reports the current value and source.

items.title: Given the current harness exposes Composer, Grok Build, Cursor/cloud, or future adapters, then a backend-listing command or probe returns stable provider IDs and role capabilities for swarm to display.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the current harness exposes Composer, Grok Build, Cursor/cloud, or future adapters, then a backend-listing command or probe returns stable provider IDs and role capabilities for swarm to display.

items.title: Given a user changes their mind after initial setup, then rerunning the policy command updates the stored backend value without editing vBRIEF story files.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a user changes their mind after initial setup, then rerunning the policy command updates the stored backend value without editing vBRIEF story files.

expected_outputs: policy tests cover persistent sub-agent backend selection and policy:show output
backend-listing probe returns stable provider IDs and role capabilities

references.title: Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch (strong-model orchestrator + cheap high-context leaf code-runners)
